## [00:00 - 03:05]

So I've got an Evergreen launch case study for you. This is with Una Duncan and Una is the founder of the FIT feels good movement. She's an award-winning fitness expert, author, and speaker on health. She specializes in helping busy women in their 40s. I think it's actually in their 40s and 50s. There it is. Fitness, fat loss, and awesomeness for women in their 40s and 50s. It's all about transforming your body, your health, and a 28-day challenge. That's her initial intro offer. It's a 28-day challenge. So Una says she started by launching her program once a month and she did that for about a year and a half. She had pretty good success but she got burnt out because she was just continually launching every single month. And since she was always focusing on her marketing, she couldn't serve her clients and create the new content that she wanted to. So then she went from every month to pulling back and only launching a few times a year assuming that those launches, those few launches would be huge and they weren't and she almost went broke. The thing is is that for her market, when people are feeling that they really need to change in her life, when they've got that pain, when they want to deal with their fitness, they want the solution now. They don't want to wait three months for the next launch. So we're gonna start it up in an Evergreen launch, an Evergreen program. And she hadn't done this before because a big part of this is she takes people through in a group, in a live group, and she's working with them for the 28 days and there's a real community element and that has a lot of power to the process and the results that people get. So she didn't want to go with Evergreen. But what she did was she went to offering a new program that starts every two weeks, but the selling of it goes on all the time. So someone might join through the Evergreen and they might have to wait up to two weeks until the next class starts, but that's okay because she's got them, she gets them to step up to make the purchase and then she can work with them for those two weeks until the challenge really gets rolling. So this is her landing page. This is sort of what the process looks like. So she's got a landing page here and she drives traffic to this landing page through Facebook. It's all Facebook ads. It's paid traffic 100%. The ads run all the time. So because she's got an Evergreen process, she can be generating leaves all the time. And when someone clicks through and they land in this page, then they click to save their seat and the webinar is actually offered within the half an hour. And then one is also offered in the evening and one the next day. So these are all recorded webinars. She's not doing them live. Of

course, when you first start doing something like this, you first put together a webinar, you want to do it live until you get the kinks figured out and then you can go to the recorded version.
